indicator.title: Outstanding liabilities (% of GSDP)
indicator.description: Stock of state-government debt outstanding at the end of each fiscal year, expressed as a share of Gross State Domestic Product. Includes loans and public-account liabilities. Higher values mean a larger debt burden relative to the state's economic base. The FRBM Act 2003 imposed the first hard ceilings on state debt; pre-2003 series used different consolidation rules.
indicator.methodology_vintage: RBI State Finances: A Study of Budgets, fetched 2026-05-11T15:18:58Z
indicator.notes: Source: RBI 'State Finances: A Study of Budgets', Statement 20 (Total Outstanding Liabilities — As per cent of GSDP). The latest two periods are the State governments' Revised Estimates (RE) and Budget Estimates (BE); earlier periods are Accounts data. Telangana's series begins in 2014-15 (state formation) — pre-2014 cells are intentionally null.
